title: PIF AI: A Multi-Tenant AI-Assisted Platform for Accelerating Cosmetic Product Information File Documentation Under Taiwan Cosmetic Hygiene and Safety Act
description:
A bilingual (Traditional Chinese / English) technical whitepaper documenting PIF AI — a multi-tenant AI-assisted platform for cosmetic Product Information File (PIF) documentation under the Taiwan Cosmetic Hygiene and Safety Act.

Coverage: the PIF 16-item regulatory structure, system architecture (FastAPI / Next.js multi-tenant SaaS), AI engine, central RAG integration (Scheme C+ isolation), security model, compliance engine (lifecycle 5 stages, business-type responsibility matrix, 14 cross-item lint rules, V0-V3 snapshots, penalty mapping, regulatory PDF generation), and — since v0.3.0 — the toxicology safety-assessment engine: NOAEL six-tier fallback cascade (read-across / structural similarity / qualitative authority / TTC), Margin-of-Safety with dermal-absorption correction, the fail-safe asymmetry doctrine, regulatory correctness (EU disclosure threshold vs concentration limit, TFDA/EU/CIR authority hierarchy, EPA ToxValDB backfill, ECHA C&L structured harvesting), and self-driving evolution with computation-basis provenance.

Languages: Traditional Chinese and English, 16 chapters + 4 appendices.

License: CC BY-NC 4.0.
keywords: Taiwan Cosmetic Hygiene and Safety Act
notes: Sibling whitepaper in the Baiyuan Tech AI Infrastructure Whitepaper Series (see also the Baiyuan GEO Platform Whitepaper, concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19994035). Prior to v0.3.x this repository's Zenodo deposits were auto-typed as 'software' because no .zenodo.json existed; this file corrects the resource type to publication/report for future releases.
